mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding its historical trend reduces equity discount rates and lifts earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and discretionary spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low federal funds rates and stable consumer price inflation compress real yields, reducing the equity risk premium and supporting higher valuations for long-duration assets over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive SPX returns do not materialize when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL remain within their respective low thresholds for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production increase exceeding 1.0 percent indicates a supply-side recovery that directly lifts cyclical sector earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises above the 1.0 percent threshold but SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising advance retail sales forecasts reflect strengthening consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS changes and SPX 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates held below 0.25 percent signal an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, lifting S&P 500 valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50 percent of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or when SPX returns are negative despite both series rising.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized alongside stabilizing consumer prices signals a soft-landing macro environment, maximizing equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5 percent and CPIAUCSL shows accelerating inflation, indicating policy tightening outweighs growth benefits.
